message: I've never been the 'prepared' person in my friend group—I'm usually the one borrowing a phone charger or asking if anyone has an umbrella. But last month when a water main break hit our neighborhood, I actually *had* a few gallons stored in the garage (don't ask me why, honestly—I think I meant to do it and just... forgot about it). Suddenly I was the one handing water bottles to neighbors, and it felt *weirdly good*. Not in a smug way, just in that quiet "oh, okay, I can actually help" way. It made me realize preparedness doesn't have to be complicated or stressful—sometimes it's just knowing what matters and keeping a little bit on hand. Which, turns out, is way easier than I thought. Have you ever been surprised to discover you were ready for something?
